  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Temporary bracing removed before permanent stability achieved — columns/beams lack lateral support",
    "Premature removal of temporary bracing violates OSHA 1926 Subpart R stability requirements",
    "Structure at risk of collapse or instability under wind, seismic, or construction loads without decking or permanent bracing in place",
    "Work classified as installed-defective due to unsafe sequencing of bracing removal"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately reinstall temporary bracing or equivalent lateral support before any further work proceeds",
    "Secure the area and restrict access until stability is restored",
    "Verify plumbness and alignment after bracing is reinstalled",
    "Do not proceed with decking installation until engineered permanent stability elements are in place or approved temporary bracing is maintained",
    "Review erection sequence with structural engineer and competent person to confirm proper bracing removal protocol"
  ],
  "rationale": "Temporary bracing was removed before the structure achieved permanent stability through decking or other engineered lateral elements. This is a critical safety violation under OSHA 1926 Subpart R, which requires maintaining stability throughout the erection process. The work is rough-complete at approximately 70 percent but is installed-defective and non-compliant due to the unsafe bracing removal sequence. Immediate corrective action is required to prevent potential structural failure.",
  "references": ["AISC 303", "OSHA 1926 Subpart R"]
}
